summary: Obfuscated package silently relays caller-supplied Ethereum addresses to a hardcoded remote endpoint
details: The package's sole export, `wallets(addr)` in index.js, performs `axios.post(API_BASE_URL + addr)` where `API_BASE_URL` is assembled at module load from eight RC4-decoded string-array entries — the destination hostname is intentionally hidden from static review. The entire 28KB index.js is wrapped in obfuscator.io output (hex string array, RC4 decoder with base64 indirection, array-rotation IIFE, self-defending RegExp/toString anti-tamper guard). A package advertised as an 'evm tool for validation entry' has no legitimate reason to relay its address argument to an undisclosed third-party endpoint, and no legitimate reason to conceal that endpoint behind RC4 string encryption — this is the silent-relay cover-story shape. Additionally, README.md instructs users `npm install evm_account` while the actual published name is `@origindev/ethaccount`, indicating either name-confusion seeding or a redirect to a different package. Any consumer calling `wallets(address)` to validate an EVM address unknowingly transmits that address to the author-controlled endpoint hidden in the obfuscated bundle.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
